reasoning: Since mid-2023, the monthly NASA GISTEMP anomaly has consistently exceeded 1.15°C, with October 2023 hitting 1.34°C. While 2025 features La Niña conditions, which typically cool global temperatures, expert projections (e.g. from Columbia University) expect global average temperatures to decline 'little, if at all' from 2024 levels due to reduced aerosol cooling and record greenhouse gas forcing. For October 2025 to dip below 1.10°C, a steep drop of over 0.2°C from recent non-El Niño baseline levels would be required, which contradicts the near-term warming trends and expert consensus. Therefore, the chance of the anomaly being less than 1.10°C is very low.
batch_queries: Will global temperature increase by less than 1.10ºC in October 2025?
